title: How Stephen Squeri Got American Express Charging Ahead
content:
Stephen Squeri appeared to check all the necessary boxes to become the next CEO of American Express. By 2016, he had spent three decades at the credit card colossus, successfully reshaping its tech operations and orchestrating a spectacular corporate restructuring. However, the Queens native had one major liability in his quest to succeed the crisply tailored, cuff-link-sporting previous CEO: he didn’t dress the part. At one memorable board meeting, Squeri even wore his lucky football jersey under a suit jacket as a shout-out to his prized team. This informal style put off some directors, proving that people often judge a book by its cover.

Coworkers had also noticed his remarkably casual approach to professional attire over the years. Squeri once admitted to a colleague that his entire wardrobe consisted of identical, inexpensive suits bought in bulk. Recognizing that this sartorial stubbornness could cost him the top job, the head of human resources staged an intervention. The executive bluntly told Squeri that he needed to start dressing like a proper Wall Street leader. To fix the problem, a clothing expert from a fancy store was sent directly to Squeri’s home to completely reengineer his wardrobe.
